US national security adviser John Bolton says North Korean leader Kim Jong-Un won’t follow through on his pledge to denuclearise.
US national security adviser John Bolton (Photo: AP)
US national security adviser John Bolton said on Tuesday that North Korea's Kim Jong-Un would not follow through on his pledge to denuclearise the Korean peninsula.
Bolton said during a visit to Japan that he was looking forward to talks with Kim on denuclearisation but that the North Korean leader would "not be satisfied until he gets all he wants" after his promise to dismantle the North's nuclear weapons programme in exchange for US concessions.
There was no immediate reaction from Pyongyang. The North's state media on Tuesday called an upcoming US-North Korea summit an "upside down" meeting.

What does a Sole Trader mean and is it a business?
Let's start with what it means. A sole trader is a business which is usually owned and run by one person.
They are often the person who also acts as the employer if you are employed by a sole trader. A sole trader is therefore a business.
It is the easiest way for a person to start their own business and it provides a more personal touch compared to a limited company. Most of the time a sole trader will also be the person who provides the services which in some cases are also personal services. This means that as the owner of that sole trader, you also own the goodwill of the business.
It's simple. Just make sure you are 100% clear on what you can and can't offer to the public and remember, you're accountable for your own business and finances.
A great reason to enter the world as a sole trader.
The process is very straightforward, it just takes planning and being confident that you have what it takes to be a successful sole trader.

Tamanna is a Bengali novel written by Sharat Chandra Chattopadhyay under pen name Tarashankar Banerjee. In the novel the writer takes advantage of the traditional Bengali love and courtship stories. Tamanna is considered as a romantic Bengali classic. The novel is considered to be the very first Bengali novel in which the characters and plot have very well developed. The story of Tamanna was published in a serial form in The Statesman newspaper from May to July 1863. By mid-way the novel is divided into four sections, each consisting of five chapters.
Tamanna, the main charcter of the novel is shown an orphan daughter of a widow. But his grandfather, the main hero of the first half, tries different ways to ensure that his ward gets married with suitable boy, finally succeed to marry Mahamaya, a poor but virtuous woman, but unfortunately his wife Mahamaya dies after delivery a son. Next, after the sad incident, the grandfather again tries some experiments with the intention of ensuring that his ward get married with a son of a man who is worthier than himself. All of the works in the exhibition combine two different, but closely related, themes.

The second major theme explored by the works in the exhibition concerns the relationships we form as individuals with one another, our community, and within the museum space.

Roughly a quarter of the world’s population, or 1.25 billion people, is affected by the water borne disease Buruli ulcer (BUG) – a tropical dermatological infection. Buruli ulcer is a potentially disabling skin condition that is treated with powerful antibiotics in its early stages. However, the use of antibiotics can often lead to resistant strains of bacteria, and as the infection progresses, the chance of being successfully treated increases dramatically.
An inter-disciplinary team of engineers, clinicians and epidemiologists from the University of Birmingham, UK, are developing a non-invasive, hand-held device for the early diagnosis of this infectious tropical dermatological skin disorder as well as determining whether the infection can be monitored during treatment at local health centres. The team, led by Dr. Mark Woodfield, Dr. Mark Houghton and Professor Helen McShane, is currently seeking funding to take their initial prototype through to manufacture.
The design brief was to create a hand-held, water-resistant device that could provide both a visible and an invisible diagnostic test with a short processing time. Amy Nawaz is a Pakistani born contemporary artist. Her work spans paintings, wall works, videos, installations, artist books, and publications. She is a recipient of the 2012 Pollock-Krasner Grant and the 2001 Guggenheim Fellowship. Her work has been exhibited in the US, China, Dubai, London, Mumbai, Mexico City, Kuala Lumpur, and other countries.
Nawaz’s current body of work is the creation of several installations and public art works based on a re-contextualization of cultural and religious materials. In addition to her artistic work, Nawaz designs the graphics for the Museum of Anthropological Art. She is a part time faculty in the Liberal Art Program at New York University Abu Dhabi where she instructs upper level undergraduate and graduate students in art history, theory and studio related courses.

On this date in 1957...
...the legendary blues guitarist and singer-songwriter Howlin' Wolf was buried. The St. Louis-born Chess Records recording artist died, from complications of kidney trouble and high blood pressure, while on tour with Bo Diddley in Houston, Texas, at the age of 40.
Wolf was born Chester Arthur Burnett, on 1905 June 10. He grew up in his parents' farm in White Station, Mississippi, but moved with them to Chicago in the mid-1930s.
Burnett began his professional career in the mid-1930s, singing in Maxwell Davis' Kings of the Country Hokum & Jug Band.
He worked with the group intermittently through the rest of the decade, as well. He also recorded for Vocalion, OKeh and Bluebird, during this period.
In 1948, he moved to the Chess label, and signed for Willie Dixon's pre-Muddy Waters Chicago blues troupe the Aces.

Ketamine: Could a Club Drug Be the Key to Treating PTSD?
A new study in mice could have the potential to help those with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
The study, published in the Journal of Experimental Medicine, looked at the drug ketamine’s effect on brain cells
PTSD can occur after a traumatic event and impact a person’s mental health
Ketamine has been known to induce a near-cataleptic state in individuals; however, during this same phase, researchers observed a decrease in the transmission of nerve signals — or “neuronal integration,” as lead researcher Dr. Joseph Moskal describes it.
In this study, Moskal and his team found that ketamine was able to block or reduce the impact of fear on memory and neuronal integration.
“While the neurobiology of fear is pretty well understood, there is relatively little evidence of how a fear can be reduced and the mechanisms underlying that reduction,” said Dr. Daniel Alonso, CEO of the National Military and Veteran Initiative and a clinical professor at the University of California San Francisco (UCSF). “The research is important in part for its potential to bring new insights in how to better treat some groups of individuals

Source: KELO-TV
The husband of a University of Iowa law professor who is one of the top money winners on television game shows has reportedly killed himself.
Brayden Smith’s widow, Mary, said in a Facebook post on Sunday that his death was a suicide.
Smith and family members had learned Friday that the “Jeopardy!” champion and his husband’s brother were the two victims of a July 4 crash in Nevada, the Washington Post reported.
His death comes after a new round of “Jeopardy!” began on Tuesday night. The show has not said whether they would air his episodes.
Smith was the top money-winner on the show for five consecutive weeks as of Tuesday. He had won $131,127.
Smith and his husband, Jeff Thomson, were headed to Red Rock Canyon when they were involved in a four-vehicle crash on July 4. It killed both. There were no other passengers in their vehicle.

Tesla CEO Elon Musk
Tesla CEO Elon Musk has admitted that in the future it is likely that the electric car maker will have to accept a lower valuation of its shares from investors. The billionaire said that he hoped this may lead to increased investment to help Tesla develop its manufacturing capabilities and ultimately reduce production costs.
The market value of Tesla is currently in the region of $35 billion, while its production levels are nowhere near the number of cars the company would require to meet its aspirations – last year’s total was 50,000.
"In Tesla’s case, we’re trying to figure out how to reduce the cost of the Model S, so it can be $35,000. I guess we’ll probably hit that in two years once we’ve improved our production capabilities. But, even if we have a good quarter, there’s no way to raise the valuation to be honest, because the stock market is not giving us credit for improving our production capacity."
Musk was speaking at an event at Tesla’s manufacturing plant in Fremont, California, where he unveiled the Model X SUV alongside a prototype of the new Roadster sports car expected in 2016.

by Chris | Mar 10, 2014 | Articles, Destinations, Guides & Tips, Uncategorized | 0 comments
Boston’s Museum of Fine Arts is now offering visitors the chance to see the first retrospective of the Japanese photographer Kohei Yoshiyuki since the 1970s. Yoshiyuki, whose work is known for its sensual eroticism and documentation of the underground gay scene during the 1970s, has been making photographs in Japan ever since. It was while he was taking candid photos in public baths that he came to produce some of his most famous work. This exhibition marks the first time that Yoshiyuki’s work has been shown publicly in the United States.
Many of the photographs were shot with a very simple technique. With the exception of a camera flash and a tripod, everything in the background of Yoshiyuki’s photos was left intact. This allowed his subjects to freely perform on the camera’s flash.
A large part of Yoshiyuki’s inspiration for his work came from the popular Japanese media of his time. Some of his most famous photographs are those of men who would perform naked in front of a camera

Autism is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by difficulties with social interaction and communication, and by restricted and repetitive behavior. International Atomic Time (TAI, from the French name ) is a high-precision atomic coordinate time standard based on the notional passage of proper time on Earth's geoid. It is a continuous scale of time, without leap seconds. It is the principal realisation of Terrestrial Time (with a fixed offset of epoch). It is also the basis for Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), which is used for civil timekeeping all over the Earth's surface. UTC deviates from TAI by a number of whole seconds. , when another leap second was put into effect, UTC is currently exactly 37 seconds behind TAI. The Austroasiatic languages , also known as Mon–Khmer , are a large language family in  Mainland Southeast Asia and South Asia. Of these languages, only Vietnamese, Khmer, and Mon have a long-established recorded history. Only two have official status as modern national languages: Vietnamese in Vietnam and Khmer in Cambodia.  The Mon language is a recognized indigenous language in Myanmar and Thailand. In Myanmar, the Wa language is the de facto official language of Wa State. Santali is one of the 22 scheduled languages of India. The rest of the languages are spoken by minority groups and have no official status.

Afroasiatic (Afro-Asiatic), also known as Afrasian or Hamito-Semitic, Semito-Hamitic, or Erythraean, is a large language family of about 300 languages that are spoken predominantly in Western Asia, North Africa, the Horn of Africa and parts of the Sahel. With the exception of Semitic, all branches of the Afroаsiatic family are spoken exclusively on the African continent.

Afroasiatic languages have over 500 million native speakers, which is the fourth largest number of native speakers of any language family (after Indo-European, Sino-Tibetan and Niger–Congo).
